source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Three identical objects, each of mass $m$, are connected by springs of spring constant $K$, as shown in Fig. 1.95. The motion is confined to one dimension. At $t=0$, the masses are at rest at their equilibrium positions. Mass $A$ is then subjected to an external time‐dependent driving force $F(t)=f \cos(\omega t)$, $t>0$. Calculate the motion of mass $C$.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Three identical objects, each of mass $m$, are connected by springs of spring constant $K$, as shown in Fig. 1.95. The motion is confined to one dimension. At $t=0$, the masses are at rest at their equilibrium positions. Mass $A$ is then subjected to an external time‐dependent driving force $F(t)=f \cos(\omega t)$, $t>0$. Calculate the motion of mass $C$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Let $x_{A}, x_{B}, x_{C}$ be the coordinates of the three masses and $a$ the relaxed length of each spring. The equations of motion are $f\cos(\omega t)+K(x_{B}-x_{A}-a)=m\ddot{x}_{A}$, $K(x_{C}-x_{B}-a)-K(x_{B}-x_{A}-a)=m\ddot{x}_{B}$, and $-K(x_{C}-x_{B}-a)=m\ddot{x}_{C}$. This set can be written as $f\cos(\omega t)=m(\ddot{x}_{A}+\ddot{x}_{B}+\ddot{x}_{C})$, $f\cos(\omega t)-2aK=m(\ddot{x}_{A}-\ddot{x}_{C})+K(x_{A}-x_{C})$, and $f\cos(\omega t)=m(\ddot{x}_{A}-2\ddot{x}_{B}+\ddot{x}_{C})+3K(x_{A}-2x_{B}+x_{C})$. Introducing the normal coordinates $y_{1}=x_{A}+x_{B}+x_{C}$, $y_{2}=x_{A}-x_{C}$, and $y_{3}=x_{A}-2x_{B}+x_{C}$, the equations become $f\cos(\omega t)=m\ddot{y}_{1}$, $f\cos(\omega t)-2aK=m\ddot{y}_{2}+Ky_{2}$, and $f\cos(\omega t)=m\ddot{y}_{3}+3Ky_{3}$. With initial conditions $x_{A}=0$, $x_{B}=a$, $x_{C}=2a$ (so that $y_{1}=3a$, $y_{2}=-2a$, $y_{3}=0$) and zero velocities, integrating the first equation gives $y_{1}=\frac{f}{m\omega^{2}}[1-\cos(\omega t)]+3a$. For $y_{2}$, trying a particular solution $y_{2}=A_{2}\cos(\omega t)+B_{2}$ leads to $A_{2}=\frac{f}{K-m\omega^{2}}$ and $B_{2}=-2a$, and the general solution $y_{2}=\frac{f\cos(\omega t)}{K-m\omega^{2}}-2a+C_{2}\cos\left(\omega_{2} t\right)+D_{2}\sin\left(\omega_{2} t\right)$ with $\omega_{2}=\sqrt{\frac{K}{m}}$, where initial conditions yield $C_{2}=-\frac{f}{K-m\omega^{2}}$ and $D_{2}=0$. For $y_{3}$, assuming a particular solution $y_{3}=A_{3}\cos(\omega t)$ gives $A_{3}=\frac{f}{3K-m\omega^{2}}$, and the general solution $y_{3}=\frac{f\cos(\omega t)}{3K-m\omega^{2}}+C_{3}\cos\left(\omega_{3} t\right)+D_{3}\sin\left(\omega_{3} t\right)$ with $\omega_{3}=\sqrt{\frac{3K}{m}}$, where $C_{3}=-\frac{f}{3K-m\omega^{2}}$ and $D_{3}=0$. Since the motion of $C$ is given by $x_{C}=\frac{y_{1}}{3}-\frac{y_{2}}{2}+\frac{y_{3}}{6}$, substituting the expressions for $y_{1}$, $y_{2}$, and $y_{3}$ yields $x_{C}=2a+\frac{f}{3m\omega^{2}}[1-\cos(\omega t)]+\frac{f}{2m(\omega_{2}^{2}-\omega^{2})}[\cos(\omega t)-\cos(\omega_{2}t)]+\frac{f}{6m(\omega_{3}^{2}-\omega^{2})}[\cos(\omega t)-\cos(\omega_{3}t)]$.
